body: Individual investors, including Ling Sun and Yulisar Khiat, made significant purchases in Indonesian companies, with Ling Sun acquiring 5.25% of PT Koka Indonesia TBK (KOKA) and Yulisar Khiat increasing his stake in PT Medikaloka Hermina TBK (MEDO) to 12.63%. Meanwhile, several companies, including PT Nippon Indosari Corpindo TBK (NIPS) and PT Pangan Sejahtera Investama, engaged in share buybacks, while others, such as PT Samuel Tumbuh Bersama and Oona Indonesia Pte Ltd, reduced their stakes in various companies.
title: Individual and institutional investors make notable transactions in Indonesian stock market.

body: PT Tera Data Indonusa Tbk (AXIO) will distribute an interim dividend of IDR 5 per share, totaling IDR 29.2 billion, to its shareholders, following approval from the board of commissioners on November 5, 2024. The company reported a net profit of IDR 43.52 billion in the first nine months of 2024 and retained earnings of IDR 152.82 billion, with its share price increasing 4.17% to IDR 125 per share in the morning session.
title: PT Tera Data Indonusa to Distribute IDR 29.2 Billion Interim Dividend to Shareholders.

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index rose 0.38% to 7,321.96 at 11 am WIB, following the Federal Reserve's decision to lower interest rates by 25 basis points, with other indices such as LQ45, IDX30, IDX80, and IDXESG also showing gains. High-market-cap issuers including Bank Rakyat Indonesia (BBRI), Barito Pacific (BRPT), and Chandra Asri Pacific (CAP) saw improvements, while others like Bank Central Asia (BBCA) and Adaro Energy Indonesia (ADRO) declined.
title: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index Rises Following Federal Reserve's Interest Rate Cut.

body: PT Jobubu Jarum Minahasa Tbk (BEER) has launched a new wine variant, Anggur Pink, which is expected to tap into a large market niche, with the total wine market in Indonesia being approximately 40 times bigger than the soju market. The company expects the new product to drive revenue growth, following a 38.8% year-on-year decline in revenue to IDR 37.83 billion in the first nine months of 2024.
title: Jakarta-based PT Jobubu Jarum Minahasa Tbk launches new wine variant Anggur Pink, eyeing extensive market potential in Indonesia.

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) fell to its lowest level in three weeks, reaching 7,317.29 by 10:30 am WIB, with several indices including LQ45, IDX30, IDX80, and IDXESGL also experiencing corrections. While blue-chip stocks such as Bank Rakyat Indonesia (BBRI), Bank Mandiri (BMRI), and Bank Central Asia (BBCA) weakened, others like Telkom Indonesia (TLKM), Indofood CBP Sukses Makmur (INDF), and Unilever Indonesia (UNVR) managed to secure an upturn.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Slumps to Three-Week Low Amid Weakness in Blue-Chip Stocks

body: PT Siloam International Hospitals Tbk (Siloam) reported a 10.8% year-over-year (YoY) increase in revenue to IDR 7.06 trillion in 9M 2024, driven by a 9.8% YoY increase in inpatient admissions and a 9.7% YoY increase in outpatient visits. The company's underlying EBITDA grew 8.2% YoY to IDR 2.11 trillion, while underlying net profit increased 10.6% YoY to IDR 977.8 billion, with hospital expansion projects expected to be completed in Q1 2025.
title: PT Siloam International Hospitals' Financial Performance Improves with 10% Increase in Revenue and Net Profit.

body: PT Petrosea TBK (PTRO) plans to initiate a 1:10 stock split, which will increase its paid-up capital to 10.08 billion shares from 1 billion shares, and reduce the share value from IDR 50 to IDR 5 per share. The plan, subject to approval at the December 16 extraordinary general meeting, aims to increase stock liquidity and transaction frequency, with implementation expected within 30 days of approval.
title: PT Petrosea to Implement 1:10 Stock Split to Increase Liquidity and Transaction Frequency

body: PT Champ Resto Indonesia Tbk (ENAK) reported a net profit of IDR 19.74 billion in the first nine months of 2024, a 35.93% year-over-year increase, driven by a 12.76% revenue growth to IDR 1.17 trillion, supported by marketing programs and outlet expansion. The company's revenue was boosted by a IDR 1 billion giveaway campaign to commemorate Gokana Ramen & Teppan's 20th anniversary, and its total outlets now stand at 327 points across 15 provinces in Indonesia.
title: Enak's Net Profit Rises 35.93% to IDR 19.74 Billion in First Nine Months of 2024

body: PT Bank Permata Tbk (BNLI) has received a rating of "IDAAA" with a stable prospect from Pemeringkat Efek Indonesia, citing solid support from its new parent entity, Bangkok Bank Public Company Limited, and a solid market position. However, the rating is limited by moderate asset quality and fierce competition in the banking industry, and may be lowered if support from its shareholder weakens or if there is a significant reduction in its portfolio.
title: Bank Permata receives "IDAAA" stable rating from Pemeringkat Efek Indonesia due to solid support from new parent entity Bangkok Bank.
